description: Workspace skill suggestions prompt JSON output.
prompts:
Skills are loaded into Claude Code to help engineers build data pipelines. Claude already knows standard methodologies from its training data. A skill must encode the delta -- the customer-specific and domain-specific knowledge that Claude gets wrong or misses when working without the skill. These skills target OLAP systems (data warehouses and lakehouses), not OLTP.

Given a Claude skill named "fabric dbt standards" of type "data-engineering". User context: Industry: SaaS, Role: Analytics engineering. Skill details: Domain: Data engineering standards; Scope: dbt models on Microsoft Fabric; Target audience: Analytics engineers; Key challenges: SCD2 snapshots and incremental materializations.

Suggest brief values for these fields. Be specific and practical, not generic.

Respond in exactly this JSON format (no markdown, no extra text):
{"description":"<Third person. Nouns must be specific. Format: '[Verb]s [specific noun(s)] [context]. Use when [one trigger].' Max 2 sentences. Topic: fabric dbt standards.>","domain":"<2-5 word domain name>","scope":"<short phrase>","audience":"<2-3 short bullet points starting with • on separate lines>","challenges":"<2-3 short bullet points starting with • on separate lines>","unique_setup":"<2-3 short bullet points starting with • on separate lines>","claude_mistakes":"<2-3 short bullet points starting with • on separate lines>","context_questions":"<exactly 2 bullets starting with • on separate lines, 2-4 words each>"}

assert.value: const match = output.match(/\{[\s\S]*\}/); if (!match) return false; const data = JSON.parse(match[0]); const fields = ['description','domain','scope','audience','challenges','unique_setup','claude_mistakes','context_questions']; fields.every((field) => typeof data[field] === 'string' && data[field].trim().length > 0) && /Use when/i.test(data.description) && /dbt|Fabric|snapshot|incremental|lakehouse/i.test(Object.values(data).join(' ')) && data.context_questions.split('\n').filter((line) => line.trim().startsWith('•')).length === 2
